Name: Banette
Ability-Effect: Prevent all effects of attacks, including damage, done to Banette by your opponent's Pokémon-ex.
Effects: If the Defending Pokémon is a Basic Pokémon, that Pokémon is now Confused.

Name: Blastoise
Ability-Effect: Each of your Active Pokémon has no Weakness.
Effects: Choose 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. This attack does 10 damage for each damage counter on Blastoise to that Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

Name: Camerupt
Ability-Effect: Any damage done to Camerupt by attacks from your opponent's Pokémon that has δ on its card is reduced by 40 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
Effects: Choose 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. This attack does 30 damage to that Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

Name: Charizard
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, when you play Charizard from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon, you may look at the top 5 cards of your deck, choose as many Energy cards as you like, and attach them to 1 of your Pokémon. Discard the other cards.
Effects: Discard all [M] Energy attached to Charizard.

Name: Dugtrio
Ability-Effect: Prevent all damage done to your Benched Pokémon by your opponent's attacks.
Effects: Choose 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. This attack does 30 damage to that Pokémon. This attack's damage isn't affected by Weakness or Resistance.
Dugtrio does 10 damage to itself.

Name: Ludicolo
Ability-Effect: If your opponent has any Pokémon-ex in play, each of Ludicolo's attacks does 30 more damage to the Defending Pokémon.
Effects: Choose 1 card from your opponent's hand without looking and discard it.

Name: Luvdisc
Effects: Count the number of your opponent's Pokémon. Search your deck for up to that number of Basic Pokémon and put them onto your Bench. Shuffle your deck afterward.

If you have a Stadium card in play, remove 1 damage counter from each of your Pokémon. If your opponent has a Stadium card in play, this attack does 10 damage to each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

Name: Manectric
Effects: Your opponent switches the Defending Pokémon with 1 of his or her Benched Pokémon, if any.
If Manectric has a Pokémon Tool card attached to it, this attack does 20 damage to each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon-ex. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

Name: Mawile
Effects: Search your deck for a Trainer card, show it to your opponent, and put it into your hand. If that card is a Pokémon Tool card, you may attach it to 1 of your Pokémon instead. Shuffle your deck afterward.
If the Defending Pokémon is Pokémon-ex, this attack does 20 damage plus 30 more damage.

Name: Sableye
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may look at the top card of your deck. Put that card back on top of your deck, or discard that card. This power can't be used if Sableye is affected by a Special Condition.
Effects: Choose 1 of the Defending Pokémon's attacks. That Pokémon can't use that attack during your opponent's next turn.

Name: Swalot
Effects: Choose either Burned or Poisoned, and either Asleep or Confused. The Defending Pokémon is now affected by both Special Conditions.
Does 50 damage plus 20 more damage for each Special Condition affecting the Defending Pokémon.

Name: Tauros
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, when you put Tauros from your hand onto your Bench, you may discard a Stadium card in play.
Effects: Search your deck for up to 2 Basic Pokémon and put them onto your Bench. Shuffle your deck afterward.

Name: Wigglytuff
Ability-Effect: If Wigglytuff is your Active Pokémon and is damaged by an opponent's attack (even if Wigglytuff is Knocked Out), the Attacking Pokémon is now Asleep.
Effects: Draw 3 cards.
If the Defending Pokémon is affected by a Special Condition, this attack does 30 damage plus 20 more damage.

Name: Blastoise
Ability-Effect: As long as Blastoise's remaining HP is 40 or less, Blastoise does 40 more damage to the Defending Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
Effects: Does 50 damage plus 20 more damage for each [W] Energy attached to Blastoise but not used to pay for this attack's Energy cost. You can't add more than 40 damage in this way.

Name: Cacturne
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), if Cacturne is your Active Pokémon, you may put 1 damage counter on 1 of your opponent's Pokémon that already has any damage counters on it. This power can't be used if Cacturne is affected by a Special Condition.
Effects: Choose 3 of your opponent's Pokémon. This attack does 10 damage to each of those Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

Name: Combusken
Effects: During your next turn, Combusken's High Jump Kick attack's base damage is 70.

Name: Dusclops
Ability-Effect: As long as Dusclops is your Active Pokémon, your opponent can't attach any Special Energy cards (except for [D] and [M] Energy cards) from his or her hand to his or her Active Pokémon.
Effects: Prevent all effects of attacks, including damage, done to Dusclops by your opponent's Pokémon-ex during your opponent's next turn.

Name: Fearow
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may search your deck for a Pokémon that has δ on its card, show it to your opponent, and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward. You can't use more than 1 Delta Sign Poké-Power each turn. This power can't be used if Fearow is affected by a Special Condition.

Name: Grovyle
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, prevent all effects of an attack, including damage, done to Grovyle during your opponent's next turn.

Name: Grumpig
Ability-Effect: Any damage done to Grumpig by attacks from [R] Pokémon and [W] Pokémon is reduced by 30 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
Effects: Does 10 damage times the number of Pokémon in play (both yours and your opponent's), excluding Grumpig.

Name: Igglybuff
Ability-Effect: You pay [C] less to retreat your Jigglypuff, Wigglytuff, Wigglytuff ex, and Igglybuff.

Name: Kingler
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
If Kingler has a Pokémon Tool card attached to it, this attack does 40 damage plus 40 more damage.

Name: Loudred
Effects: Choose 1 card from your opponent's hand without looking. Look at the card you chose, then have your opponent shuffle that card into his or her deck.
Choose 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. This attack does 40 damage to that Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

Name: Marshtomp
Effects: Does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

Name: Medicham
Ability-Effect: As long as Medicham has any [P] Energy cards attached to it, Medicham is both [P] and [F] type.
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
This attack's damage isn't affected by Resistance.

Name: Pelipper
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), if Pelipper is on your Bench, you may switch 1 of your Active Pokémon that has δ on its card with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Effects: The Defending Pokémon is now Confused.

Name: Swampert
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may draw a card. This power can't be used if Swampert is affected by a Special Condition.
Effects: This attack's damage isn't affected by Resistance.

Name: Venusaur
Ability-Effect: All Energy cards that provide only [C] Energy attached to your [G] Pokémon provide [G] Energy instead.
Effects: Does 20 damage plus 10 more damage for each [G] Energy attached to all of your Pokémon.
The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep and Poisoned. Put 2 damage counters instead of 1 on the Defending Pokémon between turns.

Name: Charmeleon
Effects: Does 10 damage plus 10 more damage for each damage counter on Charmeleon.
Discard a [R] Energy attached to Charmeleon.

Name: Charmeleon
Effects: Flip a coin. If tails, Charmeleon does 10 damage to itself.

Name: Combusken
Effects: The Defending Pokémon is now Burned.
Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 damage plus 20 more damage.

Name: Grovyle
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, prevent all effects of an attack, including damage, done to Grovyle during your opponent's next turn.

Name: Gulpin
Effects: Choose 1 of the Defending Pokémon's attacks. That Pokémon can't use that attack during your opponent's next turn.

Name: Ivysaur
Effects: The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.

Name: Ivysaur
Effects: Choose 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. This attack does 30 damage to that Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 40 damage plus 20 more damage.

Name: Lairon
Effects: Your opponent switches the Defending Pokémon with 1 of his or her Benched Pokémon, if any.
Put 1 damage counter on Lairon.

Name: Lombre
Effects: Before doing damage, discard all Trainer cards attached to the Defending Pokémon.

Name: Marshtomp
Effects: The Defending Pokémon can't retreat during your opponent's next turn.

Name: Nuzleaf
Effects: Attach a [D] Energy card from your hand to Nuzleaf.

Name: Shuppet
Effects: Search your deck for a card that evolves from Shuppet and put it onto Shuppet. (This counts as evolving Shuppet.) Shuffle your deck afterward.

Name: Skitty
Effects: Choose 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. This attack does 20 damage to that Pokémon. This attack's damage isn't affected by Weakness, Resistance, Poké-Powers, Poké-Bodies, or any other effects on that Pokémon.

Name: Wartortle
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

Name: Wartortle
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon can't attack during your opponent's next turn.

Name: Aron
Effects: Does 10 damage times the number of damage counters on Aron.

Name: Bulbasaur
Effects: The Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.

Name: Bulbasaur
Effects: Attach a [G] Energy card from your hand to Bulbasaur.

Name: Cacnea
Effects: Search your deck for a Basic Pokémon and put it onto your Bench. Shuffle your deck afterward.
Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 damage plus 20 more damage.

Name: Charmander
Effects: Does 10 damage times the number of damage counters on Charmander.

Name: Diglett
Effects: The Defending Pokémon can't retreat during your opponent's next turn.

Effects: Draw a card.

Name: Electrike
Effects: Put any 1 card from your discard pile into your hand.
Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 damage plus 10 more damage.

Effects: The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.

Name: Krabby
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 damage plus 10 more damage.

Name: Lotad
Ability-Effect: If Lotad has any [W] Energy attached to it, the Retreat Cost for Lotad is 0.

Name: Meditite
Effects: Put 2 damage counters on your opponent's Pokémon in any way you like.

Name: Mudkip
Ability-Effect: As long as Mudkip is on your Bench, prevent all damage done to Mudkip by attacks (both yours and your opponent's).

Name: Mudkip
Effects: Remove 2 damage counters from Mudkip.

Name: Numel
Effects: Numel does 10 damage to itself.

Name: Spearow
Effects: Draw a card.

Name: Spoink
Effects: Does 10 damage times the amount of Energy attached to the Defending Pokémon.

Name: Squirtle
Ability-Effect: Any damage done to Squirtle by attacks is reduced by 10 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

Name: Torchic
Effects: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

Name: Torchic
Effects: If the Defending Pokémon tries to attack during your opponent's next turn, your opponent flips a coin. If tails, that attack does nothing.

Name: Treecko
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

Name: Treecko
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused.

Name: Whismur
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused.

Name: Bill's Maintenance
Description: Shuffle a card from your hand into your deck. If you do, draw 3 cards.

Name: Castaway
Description: Search your deck for a Supporter card, a Pokémon Tool card, and a basic Energy card. Show them to your opponent, and put them into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.

Name: Celio's Network
Description: Search your deck for a Basic Pokémon or Evolution card (excluding Pokémon-ex), show it to your opponent, and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.

Name: Cessation Crystal
Description: As long as Cessation Crystal is attached to an Active Pokémon, each player's Pokémon (both yours and your opponent's) can't use any Poké-Powers or Poké-Bodies.

Name: Crystal Beach
Description: Each Special Energy card that provides 2 or more Energy (both yours and your opponent's) now provides only 1 [C] Energy. This isn't affected by any Poké-Powers or Poké-Bodies.

Name: Crystal Shard
Description: As long as this card is attached to a Pokémon, that Pokémon's type (color) is [C]. If that Pokémon attacks, discard this card at the end of the turn.

Name: Double Full Heal
Description: Remove all Special Conditions from each of your Active Pokémon.

Name: Dual Ball
Description: Flip 2 coins. For each heads, search your deck for a Basic Pokémon, show it to your opponent, and put it into your hand. If you do, shuffle your deck afterward.

Name: Holon Circle
Description: Prevent all effects of an attack, including damage, done by either player's Active Pokémon. If an Active Pokémon uses an attack, that attack ends, and discard this card.

Name: Memory Berry
Description: The Pokémon this card is attached to can use any attack from its Basic Pokémon card or any Evolution card from which the Pokémon evolved. (You still have to pay for that attack's Energy cost.) Discard this card at the end of any turn the Pokémon attacks.

Name: Mysterious Shard
Description: Prevent all effects of attacks, including damage, done to the Pokémon that Mysterious Shard is attached to by your opponent's Pokémon-ex. Discard this card at the end of your opponent's next turn.

Name: Poké Ball
Description: Flip a coin. If heads, search your deck for a Pokémon, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.

Name: PokéNav
Description: Look at the top 3 cards of your deck. You may reveal a Pokémon or Energy card you find there and put it into your hand. Put the other cards back in any order.

Name: Warp Point
Description: If your opponent has any Benched Pokémon, he or she chooses 1 of them and switches it with his or her Active Pokémon, then, if you have any Benched Pokémon, you switch 1 of them with your Active Pokémon.

Name: Windstorm
Description: Choose up to 2 in any combination of Pokémon Tool cards and Stadium cards in play (both yours and your opponent's) and discard them.

Name: Energy Search
Description: Search your deck for a Basic Energy card, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

Name: Potion
Description: Heal 30 damage from 1 of your Pokémon.

Name: Double Rainbow Energy
Description: Double Rainbow Energy can be attached only to an Evolved Pokémon (excluding Pokémon-ex). While in play, Double Rainbow Energy provides every type of Energy but provides 2 Energy at a time. (Doesn't count as a basic Energy when not in play and has no effect other than providing Energy.) Damage done to your opponent's Pokémon by the Pokémon Double Rainbow Energy is attached to is reduced by 10 (after applying Weakness and Resistance). When the Pokémon Double Rainbow Energy is attached to is no longer an Evolved Pokémon, discard Double Rainbow Energy.

Name: Aggron ex
Ability-Effect: As long as Aggron ex is your Active Pokémon, your opponent's Basic Pokémon can't attack or use any Poké-Powers or Poké-Bodies.
Effects: Choose 2 of your opponent's Pokémon. This attack doe 30 damage to each of them. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
During your next turn, Aggron ex can't use Mega Burn.
Ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Blaziken ex
Effects: Before doing damage, you may choose 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon and switch it with 1 of the Defending Pokémon. Your opponent chooses the Defending Pokémon to switch.

Before doing damage, count the remaining HP of the Defending Pokémon. If that Pokémon is Knocked Out by this attack, Blaziken ex does damage to itself equal to the attack's damage minus the remaining HP of the Defending Pokémon. This attack's damage isn't by Weakness, Resistance, Poké-Powers, Poké-Bodies, or any other effects on that Pokémon.
Ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Delcatty ex
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may use this power. Each player discards cards until that player has 6 cards in his or her hand. (You discard first.) This power can't be used if Delcatty ex is affected by a Special Condition.
Effects: Search your discard pile for all Energy cards. This attack does 10 damage times the number of Energy cards you find there. Show them to your opponent, and put them on top of your deck. Shuffle your deck afterward.
Ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Exploud ex
Ability-Effect: As long as Exploud ex is your Active Pokémon, put 1 damage counter on each of your opponent's Pokémon-ex between turns.
Effects: Discard a Special Energy card, if any, attached to the Defending Pokémon.
If the Defending Pokémon has any Poké-Powers or Poké-Bodies, this attack does 60 damage plus 20 more damage.
Ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Groudon ex
Ability-Effect: As long as Groudon ex has 1 Energy or less attached to it, damage done to any of your Groudon ex in play by attacks is reduced by 20 (after applying Weakness and Resistance). You can't use more than 1 Hard Rock Poké-Body each turn.
Effects: Discard 2 Energy attached to Groudon ex.
Ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Jirachi ex
Ability-Effect: As long as your opponent has any Pokémon-ex or Stage 2 Evolved Pokémon in play, Jirachi ex pays [C] less Energy to use Shield Beam or Super Psy Bolt.
Effects: During your opponent's next turn, your opponent can't use any Poké-Powers on his or her Pokémon.
Ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Kyogre ex
Ability-Effect: As long as Kyogre ex has 1 Energy or less attached to it, the Retreat Cost for each of your Kyogre ex is 0.
Effects: Discard 2 Energy attached to Kyogre ex. Choose 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. This attack does 70 damage to that Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Sceptile ex
Ability-Effect: Each player's Pokémon-ex can't use any Poké-Powers and pays [C] more Energy to use its attacks. Each Pokémon can't be affected by more than 1 Extra Liquid Poké-Body.
Effects: Does 60 damage plus 10 more damage for each Prize card your opponent has taken.
Ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Shiftry ex
Ability-Effect: After your opponent's Pokémon uses a Poké-Power, put 2 damage counters on that Pokémon.
Effects: Choose 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. This attack does 30 damage to that Pokémon. If that Pokémon already has any damage counters on it, this attack does 50 damage instead. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Swampert ex
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may search your discard pile for 3 Energy cards and attach them to your Pokémon in any way you like. If you do, your turn ends. This power can't be used if Swampert ex is affected by a Special Condition.
Effects: You may discard 2 cards from your hand. If you do, this attack does 60 damage plus 20 more damage and does 20 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Alakazam Star
Effects: Put any 1 card from your discard pile into your hand.

Discard a Basic Pokémon or Evolution card from your hand. Choose 1 of that card's attacks. Skill Copy copies this attack. This attack does nothing if Alakazam Star doesn't have the Energy necessary to use that attack. (You must still do anything else required for that attack.) Alakazam Star performs that attack.
Ex-Rule: You can't have more than 1 Pokémon Star in your deck.

Name: Celebi Star
Ability-Effect: If Celebi Star would be Knocked Out by damage from an opponent's attack, you may flip a coin. If heads, Celebi Star is not Knocked Out, discard all cards attached to Celebi Star, and put Celebi Star on the bottom of your deck.
Effects: Count the amount of Energy attached to Celebi Star. Put that many damage counters on 1 of your opponent's Pokémon.
Ex-Rule: You can't have more than 1 Pokémon Star in your deck.
